text: Barack Obama served as the 44th President of the United States from 2009 to 2017, having previously represented Illinois in the U.S. Senate.
The Beatles, formed in Liverpool in 1960, became the best-selling music act of all time, with estimated sales of over 600 million units worldwide.
In the 2022 FIFA World Cup final held in Qatar, Argentina defeated France in a penalty shootout after a 3-3 draw.
Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species in 1859, which introduced the theory of natural selection as the mechanism for evolution.
George Orwell, born Eric Arthur Blair in British India in 1903, wrote Nineteen Eighty-Four while living on the Scottish island of Jura.
OpenAI released GPT-4 in March 2023, a large language model that outperformed its predecessor GPT-3.5 on multiple reasoning benchmarks.
Angela Merkel, leader of the Christian Democratic Union, stepped down as Chancellor of Germany in December 2021 after 16 years in office.
Marie Curie was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903, shared with her husband Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel for their research on radioactivity.
Bob Dylan, originally from Duluth, Minnesota, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2016 for creating new poetic expressions within the American song tradition.
DeepMind, a subsidiary of Alphabet based in London, developed AlphaFold to predict protein structures using deep learning techniques.
